title: Frog Jump
hints: Notice that the jump size is determined by the previous jump, not just the current position. What two values define a unique state at each stone?
Use dynamic programming where the state tracks the jump length used to arrive at a specific stone index. A set of valid jump lengths per stone works well.
For each stone, store the set of jump sizes that can land exactly on it. Start with jump 1 at the second stone, and propagate valid jumps forward.
